The alignment model produces state of the art results in retrieval experiments on Flickr8K, Flickr30K and MSCOCO datasets.
ERNIE-ViLG 2.0 will achieve a new state-of-the-art on MS-COCO with zero-shot FID-30k score of 6.75 and significantly outperform recent models in terms of image fidelity and image-text alignment.
The Microsoft COCO Caption dataset will contain over one and a half million captions describing over 330,000 images.
Grounding DINO achieves a 52.5 AP on the COCO detection zero-shot transfer benchmark and sets a new record on the ODinW zero-shot benchmark with a mean 26.1 AP.
Convolutional image captioning technique will demonstrate performance on par with the long-short-term-memory baseline while having a faster training time per number of parameters.
DeCap outperforms other zero-shot captioning methods and unpaired captioning methods on the typical image captioning benchmarks.
The pure Transformer-based model integrates image captioning into one stage and achieves new state-of-the-art performances on the MSCOCO dataset.
Group DETR v2 achieves 64.5 mAP on COCO test-dev and establishes a new state-of-the-art on the COCO leaderboard.
SOLOIST creates new state-of-the-art results on two well-known benchmarks, CamRest and MultiWOZ, and significantly outperforms existing methods in the few-shot learning setting.
SwinBERT achieves across-the-board performance improvements over previous methods in video captioning.

VideoCapsuleNet will achieve state-of-the-art performance on multiple action detection datasets including UCF-Sports, J-HMDB, and UCF-101 with an impressive ~20% improvement on UCF-101 and ~15% improvement on J-HMDB in terms of v-mAP scores.

HERO achieves new state of the art on multiple benchmarks over Text-based Video/Video-moment Retrieval, Video Question Answering, Video-and-language Inference and Video Captioning tasks across different domains.
MAXIM will achieve state-of-the-art performance on image processing tasks while requiring fewer or comparable numbers of parameters and FLOPs than competitive models.
VideoCLIP will achieve state-of-the-art performance in zero-shot video and text understanding tasks compared to prior work and supervised approaches.
The DISC21 benchmark will promote image copy detection as an important and challenging computer vision task and refresh the state of the art.
CLIP-Italian outperforms the multilingual CLIP model on the tasks of image retrieval and zero-shot classification.
LSD-C will significantly outperform competitors on popular public image benchmarks including CIFAR 10/100, STL 10 and MNIST, as well as the document classification dataset Reuters 10K.
The proposed framework for image captioning will achieve state-of-the-art performance on standard image captioning and novel object captioning tasks.
Multimodal convolutional neural networks (m-CNNs) significantly outperform the state-of-the-art approaches for bidirectional image and sentence retrieval on the Flickr8K and Flickr30K datasets.

CoMatch achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple datasets, with substantial accuracy improvements on the label-scarce CIFAR-10 and STL-10, and a top-1 accuracy of 66.0% on ImageNet with 1% labels, outperforming FixMatch by 12.6%.
CATE achieves better performance against state-of-the-art baselines on several benchmark datasets.
The Retrieve-Copy-Generate network will outperform state-of-the-art performance in the task of Open-book Video Captioning.
SG2Caps utilizes only the scene graph labels for competitive image captioning performance.
The novel template-based method achieves an accuracy of 28.4% on the linear Dolphin18K benchmark, which is 10% (54% relative) higher than previous state-of-the-art systems.
Objects365 pre-trained models significantly outperform ImageNet pre-trained models with a 5.6 points gain based on the standard setting of 90K iterations on COCO benchmark.
HiTUT achieves over 160% performance gain in success rate compared to the previous state of the art on the ALFRED benchmark.
Human performance on the GLUE benchmark significantly outperforms the state of the art on six of the nine tasks.
TupleInfoNCE significantly outperforms previous state of the arts on three different downstream tasks.
Using CLIP as a reward function will generate more distinctive captions than the CIDEr-optimized model.
A modified baseline method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art few-shot learning algorithms on both the miniImageNet and the CUB datasets.
Lightweight convolution can perform competitively to the best reported self-attention results.
SwellShark achieves competitive scores with state-of-the-art supervised benchmarks using no hand-labeled training data.
SCAR achieves higher ROUGE scores on benchmark datasets than the existing state-of-the-art methods and baselines.
Word Mover’s Distance provides strong advantages over other metrics for image captioning evaluation.
mT5, a multilingual variant of T5, will demonstrate state-of-the-art performance on multilingual benchmarks.
The CORe50 dataset and benchmark will facilitate the evaluation and comparison of emerging techniques in continuous object recognition.
Snapshot learning leads to consistent performance improvements independent of which architecture is used.
The span-level retrieval method will outperform previous systems in various few-shot settings on the CLINC and SNIPS benchmarks.
SimPLE algorithm shows significant performance gains over previous algorithms on CIFAR-100 and Mini-ImageNet, and is on par with the state-of-the-art methods on CIFAR-10 and SVHN.
MobileBERT will be 4.3x smaller and 5.5x faster than BERT_BASE while achieving competitive results on well-known benchmarks.
The Fully Convolutional Localization Network architecture will improve speed and accuracy in dense captioning tasks compared to current state of the art approaches.
The unified weakly-supervised learning framework called TCPNet will show new state-of-the-art performance on several public benchmarks for visual information extraction.
BinaryBERT achieves state-of-the-art compression results on the GLUE and SQuAD benchmarks while being 24x smaller than the full-precision model with only a slight performance drop.

given_idea: An in-context learning-based named entity recognition approach can effectively inject in-context NER ability into pre-trained language models and recognize entities of novel types on-the-fly using only a few demonstrative instances.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: The NER-specific pre-training framework will achieve significant improvements in named entity recognition performance compared to existing pre-trained baselines.
Retrieving external contexts of a sentence can significantly improve Named Entity Recognition performance compared to using only the original sentence.
The proposed neural network model jointly learns entity mentions and their context representation, outperforming previous state-of-the-art methods on fine-grained entity type classification.
Adding context as additional sentences to BERT input systematically increases named entity recognition performance.
Using HTML tags in free text Named Entity Recognition will improve NER performance compared to Text-Only equivalents.
Pooled contextualized embeddings significantly improve the state-of-the-art for named entity recognition tasks.
Context-aware token embeddings improve named entity recognition for different types of biomedical named entities.
Named entity recognition systems may improve by designing models that explicitly operate over representations of local inputs and context, respectively.

Local Additivity based Data Augmentation (LADA) method for semi-supervised Named Entity Recognition (NER) will improve both entity and context learning by creating virtual samples through interpolation.
Gated relation network (GRN) is more capable than common convolutional neural networks (CNNs) in capturing long-term context for named entity recognition (NER).
The model augmented with hierarchical contextualized representation will establish new state-of-the-art results in named entity recognition.
The novel neural network architecture for nested named entity recognition achieves state-of-the-art F1 of 74.6, further improved with contextual embeddings (BERT) to 82.4.
The context information of multi-token entities should be more reliable in document-level named entity recognition for news articles.
Multi-Grained Named Entity Recognition (MGNER) outperforms current state-of-the-art baselines up to 4.4% in terms of the F1 score among nested/non-overlapping NER tasks.
Example-based named entity recognition will perform significantly better than the current state-of-the-art methods, especially with a low number of support examples.
A language independent method for Named Entity Recognition will outperform comparable approaches on standard datasets and enhance monolingual NER systems.
Knowledge-Augmented Language Model (KALM) learns to recognize named entities in an entirely unsupervised way by using entity type information latent in the model.
T-NER facilitates the study and investigation of the cross-domain and cross-lingual generalization ability of language models finetuned on named entity recognition.

Bidirectional Conditional Random Field (BI-CRF) improves the performance of the Named Entity Recognition (NER) model compared to an unidirectional Conditional Random Field (CRF) and backward CRF captures most difficult entities compared to the forward CRF.

In the few-shot learning setting, the proposed named entity recognition schemes significantly improve or outperform the commonly used baseline, a pre-trained language model-based linear classifier fine-tuned using domain labels.
Lexicon infused phrase embeddings will improve named entity recognition performance compared to existing methods.
Including manual features for part-of-speech, word shapes, and gazetteers can improve the performance of a neural CRF model in named entity recognition.
The character-level tagger for language-independent Named Entity Recognition will achieve close to state-of-the-art performance in seven languages using only labeled NER data.
A memory-based learner will improve performance in language-independent named entity recognition tasks.
The proposed methods outperform the nested named entity recognition state of the art on four corpora: ACE-2004, ACE-2005, GENIA and Czech CNEC.
Machine learning techniques are widely used in Named Entity Recognition and Classification (NERC) systems.
A novel Continual Learning approach for Named Entity Recognition will allow the student model to progressively learn to identify new entities without forgetting the previously learned ones.
An extension based on prototypical networks improves performance in low-resource named entity recognition tasks.

Using non-speaker annotations for building low-resource Named Entity Recognition models produces results that are consistently on par or better than cross-lingual methods built on modern contextual representations.
Enhancing Named Entity Recognition models with correlated samples will improve their efficacy on texts from special domains.
Contextual string embeddings will significantly outperform previous embeddings on sequence labeling tasks, particularly in named entity recognition for English and German.
A knowledge-based system will significantly improve the contextualized token representations for multilingual named entity recognition.
The domain adaptation method with latent semantic association (LaSA) significantly enhances the performance of named entity recognition (NER) without leveraging any labeled target domain data.
Bootstrapped text-level named entity recognition system LitNER outperforms off-the-shelf supervised named entity recognition systems.
Global entity disambiguation model based on contextualized embeddings of words and entities will achieve state-of-the-art results on standard entity disambiguation datasets.
EntityNLM can explicitly model entities, dynamically update their representations, and contextually generate their mentions, outperforming strong baselines and prior work.

A novel framework for few-shot Fine-grained Entity Typing consisting of an entity type label interpretation module and a type-based contextualized instance generator will outperform existing methods by significant margins.
Instance-based learning method will enable models to achieve high interpretability without sacrificing performance in named entity recognition.

The Convolutional Attention Network (CAN) for Chinese Named Entity Recognition (NER) outperforms state-of-the-art methods without word embedding and external lexicon resources on different domain datasets.
Correlations between contextual indicators and noun phrase descriptions of named entities can be automatically established using supervised learning.
Entity enhanced BERT pre-training method will significantly improve the performance of Chinese Named Entity Recognition compared to character-level BERT pre-trained models.

Propose-and-Refine Network (PnRNet) will achieve state-of-the-art performance on nested named entity recognition tasks by incorporating richer contextual information and eliminating errors in coarse proposals.

HunFlair outperforms other off-the-shelf biomedical named entity recognition tools with an average gain of 7.26 pp over the next best tool in a cross-corpus setting and achieves on-par results with state-of-the-art research prototypes in in-corpus experiments.
A boundary-aware neural model for nested named entity recognition will outperform other state-of-the-art methods in identifying nested entities.
Zero-shot entity typing approach will outperform state-of-the-art supervised named entity recognition systems on out-of-training datasets.
Existing methods for recognizing complex entity mentions can be categorized as token-level and sentence-level approaches.
A single Named Entity Recognition model based on multilingual BERT, trained jointly on many languages, will achieve better accuracy than models trained only on one language.

Prototypical Network will allow for effective classification of named entities with extremely limited training examples, achieving well-performing classifiers trained on only 20 instances of a target class.

Pyramid achieves state-of-the-art F1 scores in nested Named Entity Recognition on ACE-2004, ACE-2005, GENIA, and NNE, with values of 80.27, 79.42, 77.78, and 93.70 using conventional embeddings, and 87.74, 86.34, 79.31, and 94.68 using pre-trained contextualized embeddings.
Self-attention and Cross-BiLSTM can effectively remedy the XOR limitation of (CRF-)BiLSTM in modeling cross-context patterns for named entity recognition.

given_idea: A statistical hypothesis testing method will enhance text classification performance by extracting the most informative words for each given class.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: A simple text categorization model can correctly classify the hypothesis alone in about 67% of Stanford Natural Language Inference and 53% of Multi-Genre Natural Language Inference.
The SVM method gives the best results of classification of both titles and texts parts.
Likelihood ratio tests yield better results than traditional statistical methods for the analysis of text.

The naive Bayes text classifier performs very well in the standard benchmark collections, competing with state-of-the-art text classifiers based on a highly complex learning method such as support vector machine.
The Nearest Centroid Class model will improve performance in text classification by detecting unknown classes during testing.
The proposed solution obtains new state-of-the-art results on eight widely-studied text classification datasets.
Text classification algorithms will improve the accuracy of classifying complex documents in various applications.
Two Bayesian models for text classification will achieve over 80% accuracy when applied to student produced essays.
Supervised Word Mover's Distance (S-WMD) will outperform existing document metrics in text classification tasks.
A modified form of text categorization can build generally applicable classifiers for predicting reading difficulty with relatively little training data.
Class-based n-gram models can extract word classes that reflect either syntactically or semantically based groupings depending on the underlying statistics.
The simple approach for Asian language text classification without word segmentation can achieve better performance compared to traditional approaches.
The classification approach improves precision by 12% over baseline techniques that use only word-based features.
Automatically extracted keywords can improve text categorization performance when combined with full-text representation.
The proposed biased formulation of Support Vector Machine will be more accurate than existing techniques for building text classifiers using positive and unlabeled examples.
The proposed model can classify unseen categories from their textual description and significantly outperforms previous methods.
Distributional similarity and pattern-based approaches can be empirically compared for corpus-based semantic class mining.
A transition-based model with a logistic regression classifier can out-perform a majority baseline in predicting which words in a text are fixated by a reader.
Collective classification of information status will outperform previous methods in classifying fine-grained information status in written text.
The framework for building classifiers that utilize hidden topics from large-scale data collections will significantly enhance classification accuracy for short and sparse text and web segments.
The proposed approach will achieve advantageous or comparable performance in cross-lingual text classification compared to other state-of-the-art methods.
The new classification framework is effective for text classification, where the categorical structure of the data is modeled naturally within the multinomial family.

This paper attempts to move beyond the typical surface-level text classification approach by identifying semantic and psycholinguistic word classes that reflect systematic differences between men and women and finding differences between genders in the ways they use the same words.

The introduced suite of threshold-agnostic metrics will provide a nuanced view of unintended bias in text classification by considering variations in classifier score distributions across demographic groups.
A weakly-supervised method addresses the lack of training data in neural text classification and achieves inspiring performance without requiring excessive training data.
Text classifiers can facilitate causal analyses based on language data in causal inference.

The greedy, lazy, approximate, and generally efficient information-theoretic method will achieve near-optimal vocabulary coverage and measure the bits of information gained by adding one text to another.
The classifier can discriminate between Overtly Aggressive, Covertly Aggressive, and Non-aggressive texts.
A hypothesis-only model will significantly outperform a majority-class baseline across a number of Natural Language Inference datasets.

Linguistically motivated structured regularizers based on parse trees, topics, and hierarchical word clusters will improve classification accuracies compared to standard regularizers on text categorization tasks.
Text classification methods will effectively predict the law area and decisions of French Supreme Court cases.
The strategy for polychotomous classification involving estimating class probabilities for each pair of classes will improve classification performance.
The Naive Bayes model will effectively categorize lexical concepts in textual entailment tasks in an unsupervised manner.
Analyzing a small set of shallow text features for classifying individual text elements in a Web page will achieve competitive accuracy compared to complex, state-of-the-art techniques.
Employing Expectation-Maximization and active learning will reduce the need for labeled training examples in text classification while maintaining accuracy.
The proposed deep learning based approach will outperform existing state-of-the-art techniques in open-world classification of text documents.
The feature-based evaluation framework will provide a more accurate comparison of attribution methods by considering linguistic dependencies in text classification.
Predictability effects in reading result from graded activation of potentially many words, as opposed to discrete prediction of a specific word.
The proposed method combines text mining and qualitative manual content analysis to inductively develop categories from a large text corpus for content analysis.
Automatic evaluation of local explanations for text classification correlates moderately with human evaluation.
Nouns are the most influential word class in enhancing the comprehension of other words.
Sampling in many cases will improve classifier performance.
Statistical significance testing plays an important role when drawing conclusions from experimental results in NLP papers.
Maximum Entropy and N-Gram Language Model will outperform SVM and Naïve Bayes in sentiment classification of Turkish political news.
The new techniques improve the classification accuracy of the current state-of-the-art methods significantly.
The proposed discriminative training method will outperform existing state-of-the-art approaches in terms of accuracy and efficiency for text similarity measures.
Text classification methods will accurately predict the ruling of the French Supreme Court and the law area to which a case belongs.
The discriminative method for learning selectional preferences from unlabeled text outperforms other recent approaches, achieving excellent correlation with human plausibility judgments.

The integrated Open Long-Tailed Recognition algorithm will outperform existing classification approaches in terms of classification accuracy over a balanced test set that includes head, tail, and open classes.

The approach based on Formal Concept Analysis will effectively acquire taxonomies or concept hierarchies from text corpora, as evaluated by comparisons with hand-crafted taxonomies and clustering algorithms.

given_idea: BiLSTM models automatically incorporate contextual information, which is a key factor in their ability to perform well.
original: BiLSTM representations capture structural context implicitly, and this context significantly influences their performance.

top_k.sentence: BiLSTM representations capture structural context implicitly, and this context significantly influences their performance.
Context-predicting models obtain a thorough and resounding victory against count-based counterparts.
Encoding contextual information by Long Short-Term Memory and inter-sentence interactions are critical for performance in sentence pair modeling tasks.
A hierarchical BILSTM outperforms a flat BILSTM in detecting contractual obligations and prohibitions due to its broader discourse view.
The substitute-based context representation technique achieves superior performance on naturally-occurring contexts from corpora compared to previous context-based approaches.
Integrating topic information into bidirectional long short-term memory networks outperforms vanilla BiLSTMs by more than 3 percentage points in F1 in two- and three-label cross-topic settings.
The sentence-state LSTM model has strong representation power, giving highly competitive performances compared to stacked BiLSTM models with similar parameter numbers.
Existing models have surpassed the performance ceiling of the standard evaluation dataset for context-sensitive meaning representations.
A model trained with the proposed algorithm is more sensitive to the additional context.
The multi-channel model will outperform the BiLSTM-CRF baseline by effectively incorporating the tag dependency feature.
PAWS provides an effective instrument for driving further progress on models that better exploit structure, context, and pairwise comparisons.
The proposed approach based on learning to rank techniques will effectively retrieve relevant context information for a given news article.
Including context significantly improves lemmatization accuracy compared to a context-free version of the model.
The joint inference model can take advantage of contextual cues to make better predictions on objects and their relationships.
The integrated model will provide superior semantic information compared to the purely text-based approach.
Biterm topic model (BTM) will discover more prominent and coherent topics and significantly outperform the state-of-the-art baselines for short text topic modeling.
Existing models have surpassed the performance ceiling for the standard de facto dataset, i.e., the Stanford Contextual Word Similarity.
Incorporating contextual information into word representations prior to processing the task will significantly improve natural language understanding performance.
Contextual word representations derived from pre-trained bidirectional language models significantly improve end task accuracy and qualitative properties of the representations learned.
The combination of two or more kinds of contextual information gives more stable performance for automatic synonym acquisition.
Contextual features significantly impact the performance of automatic fact-checking systems.
An LSTM language model deals with lexical ambiguity in English by representing both lexical and contextual information to a large extent, but there is room for improvement for contextual information.
Recurrently controlled recurrent network (RCRN) consistently outperforms BiLSTMs and stacked BiLSTMs across various NLP tasks.
Incorporating context information into the PACRR model yields Co-PACRR, a novel context-aware neural IR model that achieves superior search results compared to established models.
EntityNLM can explicitly model entities, dynamically update their representations, and contextually generate their mentions, outperforming strong baselines and prior work.
With a conventional BILSTM-based model, it is possible to obtain fast and accurate parsers for dependency parsing.
Powerful contextualized word representations deal less well with the subtle in-context similarity relationships needed for substitution compared to models trained with this objective in mind.
The context-aware natural language generator yields significant improvements over the baseline in both automatic metrics and a human pairwise preference test.
Careful design of data collection is required to obtain image descriptions which are contextually bounded to a particular meta-level task.
Distilling knowledge from BERT into a single-layer BiLSTM can achieve competitive results in natural language processing tasks while using significantly fewer parameters and less inference time.
Social-contextual factors nurture intrinsic motivation and promote internalization, leading to desired educational outcomes.
The presented context module increases the accuracy of state-of-the-art semantic segmentation systems.
Context plays a role in learning how to disambiguate, and the type and amount of context needed varies between languages.
AutoExtend achieves state-of-the-art performance on Word-in-Context Similarity and Word Sense Disambiguation tasks.
Dependent reading bidirectional LSTM network (DR-BiLSTM) will achieve the new state-of-the-art scores on the Stanford NLI dataset for natural language inference.
Contextual LSTM models will improve performance on large scale natural language processing tasks compared to baseline LSTM models.
BERTScore correlates better with human judgments and provides stronger model selection performance than existing metrics.

Document-Context Language Models (DCLM) obtain slightly better predictive likelihoods and considerably better assessments of document coherence compared to word-level recurrent neural network language models.
A bilinear model will generate descriptive sentences for images with comparable results to state-of-the-art models.
Dynamically context-sensitive time-decay attention mechanisms significantly improve the state-of-the-art model for contextual understanding performance.
Contextual decomposition (CD) captures the contributions of combinations of words or variables to the final prediction of a long short-term memory (LSTM) network.

The knowledge, automatically mined from web-scale text corpora, enhances the triplet selection algorithm by providing it contextual information and leads to a four-fold increase in activity identification.
The feature-based model for the Constituent-Context Model achieves significant improvement on longer sentences compared to the original model.
Learning a Deep Listwise Context Model will significantly improve the state-of-the-art learning to rank methods on benchmark retrieval corpora.
Combining existing index terms of a paper with additional terms from citing papers will improve the automatic determination of the right context for indexing.
Context-sensitive optimization outperforms no or naive use of context.

A machine learning approach using linear and logistic regression models combined with document, text, and eBay specific features will effectively address the problem of keyword extraction for contextual advertisement.
The deep architecture model (DBNLR) will improve the effect of Click-Through Rate (CTR) prediction for contextual advertising compared to another deep architecture model (SAELR).
Correlations between contextual indicators and noun phrase descriptions of named entities can be automatically established using supervised learning.
Models which encode sentences as well as their local and global context perform best, significantly outperforming well-established baseline methods.

given_idea: The innovative model exhibits significant advancements over traditional methods, showcasing its capacity to learn and embody desirable attributes.
original: The proposed model achieves improvements over baselines and learns to capture desirable characteristics.

top_k.sentence: The proposed method significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art lifelong learning approaches.
The proposed model achieves improvements over baselines and learns to capture desirable characteristics.
The new set of publicly available pre-trained models will outperform the current state of the art by a large margin on a number of tasks.
Models trained on the novel feature set showed significant increases in accuracy relative to two high baselines.
Training that takes advantage of GAN-generated edited examples results in improvements in the model's ability to learn attributes compared to previous results.
The new training methods give improvements in accuracy over perceptron-trained models.
Inferring attributes is the core problem of recognition, allowing for the description of both familiar and unfamiliar objects.
A model trained using semi-supervised self-training can achieve results comparable to a model trained using a much larger set of fully labeled data.
The proposed open-world learning method based on meta-learning will effectively classify products and reject unseen classes without the need for model re-training.
The new techniques improve the classification accuracy of the current state-of-the-art methods significantly.
Overview based approaches will guide end users to select better training examples than the best performing previous design for interactive concept learning systems.
A learning system that learns cumulatively will be able to detect data from unseen classes and selectively update its models without re-training the whole system.
Employing the active learning method can significantly reduce the need for labeled training instances in both the standard inductive and transductive settings.
The learned classifiers outperform previous approaches for learning with limited data and are comparable with fully supervised classifiers trained from a small number of labeled examples.
Self-taught learning improves classification performance by using unlabeled data to construct higher-level features.
Feature feedback improves the learning process in supervised learning.
Truly human-like learning and thinking machines will have to reach beyond current engineering trends in both what they learn and how they learn it.
The proposed framework can produce comparable results compared with previous work that relies on a supervised learning-to-rank model with heavy feature engineering.
Active learning techniques have been slow to gain substantial traction in practical applications due to several important and under-discussed challenges.
The model learns active learning algorithms via metalearning for a distribution of related tasks.
The model achieves comparable performance on TimeBank-Dense without using external knowledge or manually annotated attributes of entities.
The proposed approach will support value extraction scaling up to thousands of attributes without losing performance.
Transfer learning-based approaches perform better than task-specific models trained on 3 times as much data.
Agnostic active learning algorithm A2 achieves an exponential improvement in sample complexity over traditional supervised learning methods.
Know-Evolve significantly improves performance over various relational learning approaches on large scale real-world datasets.
Attribute-based classification will enable object detection of unseen classes without requiring training images of the target classes.
The proposed transfer learning method outperforms several strong baselines, including supervised learning in the target domain and a recent transfer learning method.
The proposed method achieves 5.7% F1 improvement over a state-of-the-art supervised system which uses the full training data.
Active learning may provide benefits when used with specific models and for particular domains, but the benefits of current approaches do not generalize reliably across models and tasks.
The proposed models will outperform eight state-of-the-art models in two large-scale real-world datasets.
Co-teaching+ will demonstrate superior robustness in trained models compared to many state-of-the-art methods.
iCaRL allows learning in a class-incremental way, enabling strong classifiers and data representation to be learned simultaneously.
The Constrained Weight Space SVM outperforms both baseline supervised learning strategies and previously proposed methods for learning with labeled features.
The proposed approach significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art partial label learning approaches.
The model-agnostic meta-learning algorithm will produce good generalization performance on new learning tasks using only a small number of training samples.
A variant of the MAC model achieves comparable accuracy while training faster and transfer learning with fine-tuning results in a 15 point increase in accuracy.
Combining advanced language modeling techniques results in new state of the art performance that is significantly better than any individual model.
The proposed method learns better and converges faster than learning all the tasks in a typical multi-task learning paradigm.
Learning to rank techniques will improve model training in ranking tasks.
The proposed method is able to work in evolving environments.
The computational model will efficiently expand its previously learned concepts to new domains using a few labeled samples.
A subset of learning strategies is effective, but this effectiveness depends on the phase of the model in which they are implemented.
Active learning is helpful at larger selections with consistent gains up to 14 hours.
A dual-memory self-organizing architecture will significantly outperform current methods of lifelong learning in incremental learning scenarios.
All representation learning methods outperform state-of-the-art semi-supervised methods that do not rely on representation learning.
The semi-supervised learning model with Conditional Random Fields will effectively improve the discovery of new entities and extraction of attribute content from semi-structured data.
The proposed technique can learn a control policy faster than baseline alternatives.
Entropy-based active learning approach can significantly reduce the overall number of training examples required to reach near-optimal performance in object recognition.

The proposed knowledge representation model with entities, attributes and relations (KR-EAR) can significantly outperform state-of-the-art knowledge representation models in prediction of entities, attributes and relations.
Meta Reward Learning (MeRL) will provide more refined feedback for learning compared to alternative reward learning techniques based on Bayesian Optimization.

given_idea: Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed methodology outperforms rival benchmark methods, including those utilizing adversarial learning strategies.
original: The proposed method leads to substantial performance improvements over competitive benchmark domain adaptation methods, including methods using adversarial learning.

top_k.sentence: The proposed method leads to substantial performance improvements over competitive benchmark domain adaptation methods, including methods using adversarial learning.
Adversarial_machine_learning studies effective machine learning techniques against an adversarial opponent.

The proposed algorithm achieves comparable robustness to projected gradient descent adversarial training on the CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets at negligible additional cost compared to natural training.
The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performances for detecting both out-of-distribution and adversarial samples.
Ensemble Adversarial Training yields models with strong robustness to black-box attacks.
White-box adversarial examples are significantly stronger than their black-box counterparts in different attack scenarios.

The novel alternative adversary-generation algorithm, AddSentDiverse, significantly increases the variance within the adversarial training data, improving robustness and achieving a 36.5% increase in F1 score under adversarial evaluation.

This article presents a comprehensive review on adversarial examples in text, analyzing the advantages and shortcomings of recent adversarial examples generation methods and elaborating the efficiency and limitations on countermeasures.
A trade-off exists between robustness and accuracy in the design of defenses against adversarial examples.
Bilateral Adversarial Training (BAT) significantly improves the state-of-the-art results against adversarial attacks.
Adversarial training substantially improves the performances of target embedding models under various settings.
Adversarial examples are significantly harder to detect than previously appreciated.
Adversarial risk serves as a more accurate objective for evaluating model performance against worst-case inputs than expected risk.
Adversarial training will confer robustness to single-step attack methods and is the best for mounting black-box attacks.
Adversarial attacks are effective when targeting neural network policies in reinforcement learning, significantly degrading test-time performance of trained policies.
An adversarial backdoor embedding algorithm can bypass existing detection algorithms including state-of-the-art techniques.
Leveraging model memorization will provide evidence for membership to white-box attackers, outperforming prior black-box methods.
Adversarial attacks pose a serious threat to the success of deep learning in practice.
Ensemble-based approaches will generate transferable adversarial examples that can successfully attack black-box image classification systems.

Local Intrinsic Dimensionality can facilitate the distinction of adversarial examples and outperform several state-of-the-art detection measures for five attack strategies across three benchmark datasets.
Retrieval-Enhanced Adversarial Training (REAT) method significantly outperforms the vanilla Seq2Seq model as well as the conventional adversarial training approach.
Foolbox is a new Python package to generate adversarial perturbations and to quantify and compare the robustness of machine learning models.
The iterative procedure for augmenting the dataset with adversarial examples will improve model performance across a range of a priori unknown target domains.
Max-Margin Adversarial (MMA) training will achieve adversarial robustness by directly maximizing the margins for each datapoint.
Adversarial logit pairing improves accuracy on adversarial examples over vanilla adversarial training and achieves state of the art defense on ImageNet against PGD white box attacks.
Crafting adversarial examples for sequence-to-sequence models will demonstrate that these models are intrinsically more robust to adversarial attacks compared to CNN-based classifiers.

Learning prompts by gradient descent will significantly improve the performance of language models across multiple tasks.
Context-tuning improves natural language generation by deriving contextualized prompts based on input text and utilizing continuous inverse prompting.
Automatically generated prompts are a viable parameter-free alternative to existing probing methods for eliciting knowledge from masked language models.
Least-to-most prompting is capable of generalizing to more difficult problems than those seen in the prompts.
Black-box Discrete Prompt Learning will efficiently adapt pre-trained language models across different downstream tasks without fine-tuning the model in the cloud.
Higher quality prompts result in higher quality generations of free-text explanations.
Including explanations in the prompts for large language models yields small to moderate accuracy improvements over standard few-shot learning.
Finetuning language models in the few-shot setting can considerably reduce the need for prompt engineering and achieve competitive accuracy with null prompts.
The prompt-based method (PTG) for text generation will yield competitive or better results than fine-tuning methods.
Inverse prompting enhances the relevance between the prompt and the generated text, thus improving controllability in text generation.
Combining pretrained large language models' in-context learning abilities with scratchpad prompting results in a dramatic improvement in length generalization.
Prompt transfer is promising for improving prompt tuning in natural language processing.
Language models can explicitly infer an underlying task from a few demonstrations by prompting them to generate a natural language instruction that fits the examples.
0-shot prompts can significantly outperform few-shot prompts in mapping large generative language models to supervised tasks.
The novel taxonomy of prompt modifiers provides researchers a conceptual starting point for investigating the practice of text-to-image generation.
Prompt-based learning allows the language model to perform few-shot or zero-shot learning, adapting to new scenarios with few or no labeled data.

Multi-stage prompting approach will generate knowledgeable responses from a single pretrained language model, outperforming state-of-the-art retrieval-based models and finetuning-based dialogue models in terms of knowledge relevance, correctness, and engagement.
Contrastive demonstration tuning will improve the performance of prompt-tuning in pre-trained language models compared to existing methods.
RLPrompt optimizes discrete prompts for language models using reinforcement learning, enhancing training efficiency and performance across various tasks.
Unified Prompt Tuning (UPT) framework will lead to better few-shot text classification for BERT-style models by explicitly capturing prompting semantics from non-target NLP datasets.
PromptIDE allows users to experiment with prompt variations, visualize prompt performance, and iteratively optimize prompts.
Prompt-based models do not understand task instructions in ways analogous to humans' use of task instructions.
Input-tuning fine-tunes both the continuous prompts and the input representations, leading to a more effective way to adapt unfamiliar inputs to frozen pretrained language models.
Codex language model performs better than state-of-the-art models when provided with a small number of in-domain examples in the prompt.
An iterative context-aware prompter will effectively elicit relevant knowledge from pre-trained language models for multi-step inference.
Prompt-based learners can successfully be applied in true few-shot settings.
PromptChainer supports building prototypes for a range of applications.
The black-box prompt tuning framework for vision-language models outperforms both hand-crafted prompt engineering and gradient-based prompt learning methods.
Prompt-based probing may be inaccurate, inconsistent, and unreliable for evaluating pretrained language models.

OpenPrompt is a research-friendly framework that is equipped with efficiency, modularity, and extendibility, allowing users to combine different pre-trained language models, task formats, and prompting modules in a unified paradigm.
Prompt-to-prompt editing framework will enable high-quality image synthesis and fidelity to edited prompts by controlling edits through text only.
Few-shot prompt-based models exploit superficial cues, performing well on instances with superficial cues but underperforming on instances without them.
The strategies for effective human-computer interaction will improve the experiences of novice and expert users.
Predicting helpfulness based on automatically detected linguistic features of the suggestions will enhance the support for creative writing.
A novel prompt-learning based framework will enhance cross-lingual natural language inference by reformulating the problem to a masked language modeling problem using cross-lingual templates.
Dual context-guided continuous prompt tuning method will outperform existing prompt tuning methods in few-shot learning tasks.

An efficient method for retrieving prompts for in-context learning using annotated data and a language model will substantially outperform prior work and multiple baselines across sequence-to-sequence tasks.
The unified prompt will significantly outperform the strong baselines across different languages.
Natural language generation systems can help Wikipedia editors bootstrap new articles by generating introductory sentences that score well in terms of perceived fluency and appropriateness.
Incorporating external knowledge into the verbalizer will improve and stabilize prompt-tuning for text classification.
Tailor will enhance attribute-based controlled text generation by utilizing a prompt-based approach that allows for multi-attribute text generation without re-training.
Prompting provides a benefit in fine-tuning pretrained models for classification, often worth hundreds of data points on average across classification tasks.
Grounding high-level tasks expressed in natural language to actionable steps can be effectively achieved by large language models when prompted appropriately.
Natural language prompts allow us to influence properties like formality or specific dialect of the output in machine translation models.
Hierarchical story generation will significantly improve the relevance of generated stories to prompts compared to non-hierarchical models.
P-Tuning employs trainable continuous prompt embeddings in concatenation with discrete prompts to stabilize training and improve performance on natural language understanding tasks.

Current frozen-model techniques such as prompt tuning are only the tip of the iceberg, and more powerful methods for leveraging frozen language models can do just as well as fine tuning in challenging domains without sacrificing the underlying model's versatility.
Comprehensive Instruction (CINS) will consistently improve techniques that finetune pre-trained language models with raw input or short prompt in task-oriented dialog systems.

given_idea: By utilizing the MIMIC-RRS dataset, researchers can develop and evaluate radiology report summarization models that are capable of adapting to diverse anatomies and modalities, thereby improving their overall effectiveness and factual correctness.
original: Optimizing a neural summarization model with a factual correctness reward via reinforcement learning will substantially improve the factual correctness and overall quality of outputs for radiology summaries.
